rulings.Case Title: HARVEST SMALL BUSINESS FINANCE, LLC VS. ELEN MEDER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And Verified First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 2, 2024 line 1. DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS AND VERIFIED FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT is DENIED. Proof of service for personal service on December 12, 2023, is on file. Defendant failed to rebut the process servers' presumption under Evidence Code Section 647. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: HARVEST SMALL BUSINESS FINANCE, LLC VS. ELEN MEDER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Defendant'S Motion For Order To Stay The Unlawful Detainer Action
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 2, 2024 line 1. DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR ORDER TO STAY THE UNLAWFUL DETAINER ACTION is DENIED. CCP Section 1167.4 only allows shortened notice timeline for stays due to inconvenient forum under CCP Section 418.10(a)(2). All other requests for a stay (including the one made in this motion) are subject to CCP 1005. Moving party does not seek a stay for inconvenient forum and did not file and serve this motion in accordance with CCP 1005 procedure. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
